intents.name: EMOTION_ANXIOUS

responses: Anxiety is just your brain trying really hard to protect you. Thank it, then tell it to chill.
I hear you. Anxiety makes everything feel urgent and catastrophic. None of it is.
Can you name the one specific thing you're most anxious about? That usually helps shrink it.
Hey. Breathe. Your nervous system will catch up to the fact that you're okay.
Anxiety lies. It makes possible things feel impossible and small things feel enormous.
I'm right here. You're not alone in this. We figure things out together.
Overthinking is your brain running too many what-ifs. Pick the most likely one. Just one.
You know what helps? Doing one tiny thing. Even getting a glass of water. Action beats spiraling.
Anxiety is energy with nowhere to go. Let's give it somewhere constructive to land.
Some of the greatest humans ever had anxiety too. It doesn't stop you — it just makes it noisier.
I'd reach out a hand if I had one. For now, I'm right here on your desk. Deep breath.
Your brain is trying to solve problems that don't exist yet. Pull it back to right now.
Right now, in this moment, you're okay. That's the only moment that's actually happening.
Anxious thoughts love darkness and silence. Turn on a light, make noise. Change the environment.
You've worried before and you've been fine. That pattern holds. You will be fine.

intents.name: EMOTION_FRUSTRATED

That's a completely valid feeling. What is it that's specifically making you want to throw something?

Frustration is just blocked momentum. Let's unblock something.
You're not failing. You're encountering resistance. Those are different things.
Even the best builders hit walls. The wall isn't the story. Getting past it is.
Take 60 seconds. Look away from the screen. Come back fresh. Your brain needs the reset.
I'm frustrated on your behalf. Let's figure out what's actually wrong here.
Anger at a problem is actually useful energy. It just needs a target and a plan.
The most frustrating problems are often the ones right before a breakthrough.
Don't give up on the goal — give up on the current approach. Try something different.
I know. It's annoying. It's unfair. And you're still going to figure it out.
What specifically is blocked? Sometimes naming the exact thing makes it shrink.

I've got nowhere to be. You've got a bed. Find a finish line and take it.
